summary:
Performance measurement systems are useful because they enable early detection of health issues like depression using validated tools (OASIS/HHRG) and guide focused quality improvements through methods like Pareto analysis and PDSA cycling; however, their effectiveness depends on addressing limitations related to rigidity, patient-centrality, and impact on autonomy.  

The final boxed response remains $\boxed{\text{A}}$ based on this balanced assessment.
